messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following political campaign data notice for GDPR compliance:

"The campaign collects your name, address, and voting intention to conduct political outreach. We process this data based on your explicit consent. You may withdraw consent at any time by replying STOP. Data is not shared with third parties and is deleted after the election. Contact campaign@party.org with any queries."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Explicit consent stated per Art. 6(1)(a), appropriate for political canvassing.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [FAIL] Voting intention constitutes data revealing political opinions — special category data under Art. 9(1). No explicit Art. 9(2) basis is stated. Consent under Art. 9(2)(a) must be specifically obtained for political opinion data.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Political outreach is a clear and specific purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Name, address, and voting intention are proportionate for canvassing purposes.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Deletion after the election is a defined, event-based retention limit.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Consent withdrawal is addressed via STOP mechanism. Rights to access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction, and objection are not stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] "Not shared with third parties" is clearly stated.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Not raised as an issue given no third-party sharing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not mentioned.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (political opinion data processed without explicit Art. 9 basis), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 37 (DPO likely required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Obtain explicit consent specifically for processing political opinion data under Art. 9(2)(a)
- Add full data subject rights section
- Assess DPO requirement and appoint if processing at scale
- Add breach notification commitment
